## 🌎 Geopolitics

### Xi Warns Trump That Taiwan Could Trigger US-China "Conflict" at Beijing Summit

President Trump and Xi Jinping concluded their first round of talks in Beijing on May 16, with Xi stating explicitly that Taiwan is the most important issue between Washington and Beijing, cautioning that mishandling it could lead to confrontation or conflict between the two powers. The summit also covered trade, the Middle East, Ukraine, and the Korean Peninsula, with both leaders agreeing that the Strait of Hormuz must remain open and that Iran cannot develop nuclear weapons. Trump emerged framing the talks as a dial-down of trade war tensions, with the US having agreed to cut tariffs on Chinese goods to 30% while China reciprocated with 10% tariffs on US products.

**Why it matters.** This summit matters for India's macro picture in three ways. First, Trump-Xi détente directly affects US posture toward Indo-Pacific alliance-building—India's strategic pivot and defense partnerships hang partly on US-China friction levels. Second, the Strait of Hormuz agreement signals Washington and Beijing are coordinating energy security, which ripples through to India's own crude imports and inflation dynamics, especially after the US-India sanctions-compliance trade deal in February that penalized Russian oil imports. Third, the 90-day assessment window Trump and Xi established creates a clear decision point for whether tariff relief holds—if it collapses, global supply chains (including India's semiconductor and electronics sectors) face renewed volatility.

**Action.** Watch the 90-day reassessment timeline closely—May 16 through August marks a critical window for whether this truce sticks. Read the CNBC and CBS News accounts of the summit outcomes to understand where Trump sees wins (agricultural sales, Boeing deals) versus where tensions remain (rare earths, semiconductors). For India exposure, monitor RBI commentary on crude price impacts and track any official US statements on the Russia oil sanction framework—the February deal's market-access carrot was conditional on India not resuming Russian oil imports, and energy market stability will test that framework.

*Sources: [[https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-xi-jinping-meeting-china-beijing-trade-tariffs-taiwan-iran/](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-xi-jinping-meeting-china-beijing-trade-tariffs-taiwan-iran/)]([https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-xi-jinping-meeting-china-beijing-trade-tariffs-taiwan-iran/](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-xi-jinping-meeting-china-beijing-trade-tariffs-taiwan-iran/)) · [[https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/14/trump-xi-summit-us-china-trade-taiwan-iran-nvidia.html](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/14/trump-xi-summit-us-china-trade-taiwan-iran-nvidia.html)]([https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/14/trump-xi-summit-us-china-trade-taiwan-iran-nvidia.html](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/14/trump-xi-summit-us-china-trade-taiwan-iran-nvidia.html)) · [[https://www.democracynow.org/2026/5/14/us_china](https://www.democracynow.org/2026/5/14/us_china)]([https://www.democracynow.org/2026/5/14/us_china](https://www.democracynow.org/2026/5/14/us_china))*

---

### Strait of Hormuz Energy Crisis Hits "Greatest Threat in History" as US Sanctions License Expires

The Strait of Hormuz, which normally handles 20-25% of global seaborne oil and 20% of LNG trade, has seen flows collapse to a fraction of normal levels, with the International Energy Agency declaring the crisis "the greatest global energy security threat in history"—surpassing even the 1970s oil shocks in volumes affected. The situation escalated as the US General License 134B, which permitted Russian oil transactions through May 16, 2026, expired on that exact date, removing a temporary sanctions relief valve that had helped stabilize markets. Simultaneously, OPEC+ has begun unwinding voluntary production cuts totaling nearly 4 million barrels per day, while the IEA projects global oil supply could exceed demand by 2 million barrels per day in 2026.

**Why it matters.** For Shashank's India lens, this is a collision of three macro forces. First, India's economy is deeply energy-import dependent—crude prices directly feed inflation and RBI policy, both of which matter for your market exposure and consulting work. Second, India's February 2026 trade deal with the US tied market access to sanctions compliance, specifically penalizing Russian oil imports; the expiration of the license removes the safety valve that Washington had allowed, putting pressure on India's energy sourcing strategy and India-US relations. Third, OPEC+ unwinding cuts into a potential oversupply environment is deflationary but creates new geopolitical fragmentation—OPEC may tighten production again to support prices, which reshapes energy corridors and trade relationships that India depends on.

**Action.** Monitor WTI and Brent crude weekly to see if the Hormuz crisis tightens supply enough to offset OPEC+ cuts—the spread between the two will signal whether Asia's crude import costs rise. Check Atlantic Council's Energy Sanctions Dashboard for the latest on sanctions-driven supply redirection. Ask your India contacts in energy/trading whether they're seeing pressure on sourcing strategy post-May 16 license expiry—this will be a leading indicator of India-US trade friction.

*Sources: [[https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/energy-sanctions-dashboard/](https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/energy-sanctions-dashboard/)]([https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/energy-sanctions-dashboard/](https://www.atlanticcouncil.org/energy-sanctions-dashboard/)) · [[https://markets.financialcontent.com/wral/article/marketminute-2026-1-2-the-2026-oil-standoff-geopolitical-sanctions-clash-with-a-looming-global-surplus](https://markets.financialcontent.com/wral/article/marketminute-2026-1-2-the-2026-oil-standoff-geopolitical-sanctions-clash-with-a-looming-global-surplus)]([https://markets.financialcontent.com/wral/article/marketminute-2026-1-2-the-2026-oil-standoff-geopolitical-sanctions-clash-with-a-looming-global-surplus](https://markets.financialcontent.com/wral/article/marketminute-2026-1-2-the-2026-oil-standoff-geopolitical-sanctions-clash-with-a-looming-global-surplus)) · [[https://www.weforum.org/stories/2026/04/geopolitics-of-energy-what-leaders-are-saying/](https://www.weforum.org/stories/2026/04/geopolitics-of-energy-what-leaders-are-saying/)]([https://www.weforum.org/stories/2026/04/geopolitics-of-energy-what-leaders-are-saying/](https://www.weforum.org/stories/2026/04/geopolitics-of-energy-what-leaders-are-saying/))*

## 🧠 Artificial Intelligence

### Thinking Machines Lab Ships Full-Duplex AI That Listens While It Speaks, Ditches the Turn-Taking Model Entirely

Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab published a research preview of what it calls Interaction Models on May 11, 2026, announcing TML-Interaction-Small — a 276B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with only 12B parameters active per forward pass. The architecture is full-duplex: it processes continuous audio, video, and text streams simultaneously without voice-activity detection harnesses, turn boundaries, or stitched component pipelines. Response latency is reported at 0.4 seconds, with 200ms micro-turns replacing the standard request-response loop.

**Why it matters.** Every production voice AI today is secretly two or three glued-together systems — a VAD to detect speech end, an ASR model, the LLM, a TTS synthesizer — with the seams showing whenever a user interrupts. Thinking Machines' architecture dissolves that stack by training a single model on continuous multimodal streams from scratch. The dual-model split — a live "interaction model" that stays resident and a background model handling reasoning and tool calls asynchronously — is the key engineering insight: you get low-latency responsiveness without sacrificing reasoning depth, because the heavy compute happens off the critical path. For engineers building voice agents, this matters because it eliminates the barge-in problem, the awkward silence before a response, and the context-loss that happens when VAD cuts audio mid-sentence. The 12B-active-parameter efficiency on a 276B MoE also points at a real inference cost story if the architecture holds up to independent testing.

**Action.** The research preview is accepting limited sign-ups. Before building or buying a voice pipeline in 2026, read the Thinking Machines architecture writeup (linked via TechCrunch and MarkTechPost below) and specifically stress-test whether your current VAD + ASR + LLM stack can handle barge-in at latency under 500ms. If it cannot, the Interaction Model architecture is the concrete alternative to evaluate once wider access opens. The 276B/12B-active MoE ratio is worth benchmarking directly against your current inference costs.

*Sources: [techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/11/thinking-machines-wants-to-build-an-ai-that-actually-listens-while-it-talks/) · [marktechpost.com](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/05/13/mira-muratis-thinking-machines-lab-introduces-interaction-models-a-native-multimodal-architecture-for-real-time-human-ai-collaboration/) · [theaiinsider.tech](https://theaiinsider.tech/2026/05/12/mira-muratis-thinking-machines-lab-unveils-full-duplex-ai-that-responds-in-0-4-seconds/)*

---

### Google I/O Opens Tuesday — Gemini 4 Expected to Land on an ARC-AGI-2 Leaderboard Where GPT-5.5 Currently Sits at 85%

Google I/O 2026 keynote is scheduled for May 19 at 10 AM PT, with Gemini 4 as the expected centerpiece. As of May 13, the ARC-AGI-2 leaderboard — the benchmark (measuring novel abstract pattern extrapolation, not retrieval) that current frontier models genuinely struggle with — shows GPT-5.5 leading at 85%, GPT-5.4 Pro at 83.3%, and Gemini 3.1 Pro at 77.1%. Gemini 3 Deep Think previously posted 84.6% on ARC-AGI-2 in February, and Gemini 4 is expected to push that number higher. Pre-I/O reporting suggests a 2 million token context window and integrated image/video generation as distinguishing features.

**Why it matters.** The ARC-AGI-2 delta between 77.1% (Gemini 3.1 Pro) and 85% (GPT-5.5) is not cosmetic — it reflects genuine reasoning-under-novelty gaps that show up in multi-step agentic tasks where the model must compose rules it has not seen before. If Gemini 4 clears or surpasses GPT-5.5 on ARC-AGI-2, that changes the model-selection calculus for agentic workflows where reasoning robustness matters more than throughput. The 2M-token context figure is the more immediately practical number: at that size, a large production codebase fits in a single context without retrieval augmentation, which eliminates the retrieval-chunk accuracy problem for code generation agents. For a consulting practice billing on AI-assisted software development, that is the capability shift worth pricing into engagements right now.

**Action.** Watch the May 19 keynote live or read the Gemini 4 model card when it drops. Immediately run your standard eval suite — whatever you use to benchmark code generation, multi-hop retrieval, and instruction following — on Gemini 4 against your current default model. Do not rely on Google's internal benchmark numbers without cross-checking on RULER, MRCR v2, and your own task distribution. The current leaderboard data at lmcouncil.ai/benchmarks is the cleanest independent comparison point to use as your baseline before Tuesday.

*Sources: [androidheadlines.com](https://www.androidheadlines.com/2026/05/google-io-new-gemini-model-launch-gpt-5-5-rival.html) · [sources.news](https://sources.news/p/google-about-to-release-new-gemini) · [lmcouncil.ai](https://lmcouncil.ai/benchmarks)*

## 🇮🇳 India

### Rapido Captures $240 Mn in Prosus-Led Round as Capital Gets Surgical About Indian Startups

Rapido raised $240 million in the week ending May 16, 2026, with Prosus leading the round alongside WestBridge Capital and Accel. The ride-hailing and logistics company accounted for roughly 79% of the $303 million Indian startups raised that week, signaling that while headline numbers look robust, institutional capital is narrowing focus to companies with proven operating metrics and defensible positions. Outside Rapido's mega-round, capital remained highly selective, flowing primarily to companies with hardware IP, consumer moats, or regulated compliance deep.

**Why it matters.** For engineers building in India's startup ecosystem, this validates a hard truth: the era of funding generalism is over. Capital is flowing to companies that have already proven unit economics, which means your consulting gig or early-stage raises depend on demonstrating traction, not just thesis. For someone evaluating where to join or what metrics to hit before the Series A pitch, Rapido's ability to command a nine-figure round shows that consumer logistics with strong retention and network effects still unlock patient capital — but only if you've already built the operational machine.

**Action.** Track Rapido's go-to-market in new verticals outside its core auto-taxi business; review Prosus's other Indian tech bets to understand what quant-driven capital is optimizing for. If you're raising, demand numbers on unit economics, CAC payback, and net dollar retention before the pitch.

*Sources: [[Indian Startup Funding Roundup \]([Indian Startup Funding Roundup \) · [May 16, 2026: Rapido Leads A $303 Mn Week](https://www.bhavyasharmaandassociates.com/indian-startup-funding-roundup-may-16-2026/)](May 16, 2026: Rapido Leads A $303 Mn Week](https://www.bhavyasharmaandassociates.com/indian-startup-funding-roundup-may-16-2026/)) · [[Startups in India - 2026 Latest Funding Rounds](https://tracxn.com/d/geographies/india/__ujYf3QI9FSnpS3x-zJCSwnay2nENQhm1kAN-U8-6Kfg)]([Startups in India - 2026 Latest Funding Rounds](https://tracxn.com/d/geographies/india/__ujYf3QI9FSnpS3x-zJCSwnay2nENQhm1kAN-U8-6Kfg)) · [[Indian Startups Funding and Investors Data for 2026](https://startuptalky.com/indian-startups-funding-investors-data-2026/)]([Indian Startups Funding and Investors Data for 2026](https://startuptalky.com/indian-startups-funding-investors-data-2026/))*

---

### Krutrim Hits Profitability on ₹300 Crore Revenue; India's AI Unicorn Pivots From Models to Cloud Infrastructure

Krutrim, Ola founder Bhavish Aggarwal's AI venture and India's first AI unicorn, reported ₹300 crore in FY26 revenue (a threefold increase year-over-year) and achieved its first annual net profit with margins exceeding 10 percent. The company formally shifted strategy away from foundation model development and chip design toward building AI cloud infrastructure, a realignment that followed a deliberate capital and talent reallocation in late 2025. Krutrim is now financially self-sustaining with no immediate external funding needs, even from Aggarwal.

**Why it matters.** This is the clearest signal yet that India's path to AI economics runs through applied infrastructure, not closed-source models. For engineers evaluating whether to chase LLM labs versus infrastructure plays, Krutrim's rapid pivot to cloud services and near-term profitability suggests the market rewards builders who deliver utility to Indian enterprises over those building consumer-facing AI wrappers or competing on foundation models. The 10%+ margin achievement at scale shows that AI infrastructure can be locally sustainable without permanent VC subsidy — a crucial insight for engineers deciding whether to stay bootstrapped or take institutional capital.

**Action.** Review Krutrim's published case studies and customer success metrics for its AI cloud offering; model your own infrastructure unit economics against similar 10%-15% target margins. If your startup competes with Krutrim or builds adjacent infrastructure, track their pricing and customer acquisition strategy as a benchmark for Indian market appetite.

*Sources: [[Krutrim reports first profit, triples revenue as it pivots to AI cloud \]([Krutrim reports first profit, triples revenue as it pivots to AI cloud \) · [Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/companies/start-ups/krutrim-reports-first-profit-triples-revenue-as-it-pivots-to-ai-cloud-126050401703_1.html)](Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/companies/start-ups/krutrim-reports-first-profit-triples-revenue-as-it-pivots-to-ai-cloud-126050401703_1.html)) · [[Krutrim announces strategic pivot to AI cloud services \]([Krutrim announces strategic pivot to AI cloud services \) · [YourStory](https://yourstory.com/ai-story/krutrim-announces-strategic-pivot-to-ai-cloud-services)](YourStory](https://yourstory.com/ai-story/krutrim-announces-strategic-pivot-to-ai-cloud-services)) · [[Krutrim Pivots to AI Cloud and Posts First Profit in FY26 \]([Krutrim Pivots to AI Cloud and Posts First Profit in FY26 \) · [Entrackr](https://entrackr.com/news/krutrim-pivots-to-ai-cloud-clocks-rs-300-cr-revenue-in-fy26-11800907)](Entrackr](https://entrackr.com/news/krutrim-pivots-to-ai-cloud-clocks-rs-300-cr-revenue-in-fy26-11800907))*

## 📈 Markets

### Global Risk-Off on Trump-Xi Stalemate; Crude Surges Past $107 as Middle East Tensions Flare

US equities rolled over sharply on Friday as the Trump-Xi summit concluded without major policy breakthroughs. The S&P 500 fell 1.24% to 7,408.50, Nasdaq dropped 1.54% to 26,225.14, and the Dow lost 1.07% to 49,526.17. Brent crude surged through $107 per barrel on supply-disruption fears from Middle East tensions, while gold sold off to $4,530 per ounce, down 4% on the week. In India, the Nifty 50 closed at 23,643.50 on May 15, down 0.19% — a modest buffer that reflects India's lower geopolitical exposure relative to global energy markets.

**Why it matters.** The macro frame has shifted from growth optimism to stagflation fears. Elevated crude means imported cost-push inflation for India, narrowing the RBI's policy space even as real rates already compress. The April CPI reading of 3.48% sits comfortably within the RBI's 2–6% band, but oil at $107+ will push headline prints higher in May–June. This repricing will pinch Indian exporters and AI-infra capex budgets: both leverage global growth narratives that are now being questioned. For prop traders, the correlation between UST yields and EM equity valuations just tightened, reducing spread opportunities.

**Action.** Watch Brent hold-or-break at $105; a drop below signals the shock is priced. Today's focus shifts to Fed speakers: any hint of rate-cut delays will crush momentum stocks further. Track RBI's June review calendar—oil inflation will dominate the narrative, possibly forcing a hawkish hold or delayed easing cycle. USD/INR at 95.89 is a yellow flag for rupee weakness if crude stays elevated.

*Sources: [[S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow closes](https://www.thestreet.com/latest-news/stock-market-may-14-2026-sp-futures-rise-after-closing-at-record-high)]([S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow closes](https://www.thestreet.com/latest-news/stock-market-may-14-2026-sp-futures-rise-after-closing-at-record-high)) · [[Brent crude and gold prices](https://fortune.com/article/price-of-oil-05-14-2026/)]([Brent crude and gold prices](https://fortune.com/article/price-of-oil-05-14-2026/)) · [[Nifty 50 close](https://in.tradingview.com/symbols/NSE-NIFTY/)]([Nifty 50 close](https://in.tradingview.com/symbols/NSE-NIFTY/)) · [[India inflation framework](https://ddnews.gov.in/en/rbi-projects-inflation-at-2-1-in-fy26-revises-fy27-growth-outlook-upward/)]([India inflation framework](https://ddnews.gov.in/en/rbi-projects-inflation-at-2-1-in-fy26-revises-fy27-growth-outlook-upward/))*

---

### UST 10Y Breaks to 1-Year High at 4.6%; India G-Sec Reprices to 7.1% as Capital Flows Reverse

US 10-year Treasury yields jumped 10 basis points to 4.6%, marking a one-year high as inflation expectations re-anchor on crude and geopolitical shock. India's 10-year G-Sec yield rebounded to 7.1%, erasing recent dovish bets as global real rates reprice. The USD/INR pair held at 95.89, but the pressure is building on the rupee's carry appeal relative to tightening EM spreads.

**Why it matters.** This is a valuation reset with immediate implications for capital allocation. Higher UST yields pull the risk-free rate higher, compressing multiples on growth equities—exactly where India's AI and fintech names are concentrated. For consulting and quant research, the re-coupling of US yields to EM spreads creates duration hedging costs. Cross-currency basis swaps will widen, raising the cost of dollar funding for Indian corporates. The 2s10s curve inversion risk is rising; if 10Y breaks 4.8%, duration trades unwind aggressively, and emerging-market credit will see spread blowouts.

**Action.** Monitor if G-Sec yield breaks 7.2%—that would signal real-rate reassessment in India and likely trigger RBI communication. Track the 10Y UST level at 4.65; a close above is a technical break that could extend to 4.8%. For prop desks, this is a volatility expansion signal: convexity trades and curve flatteners become profitable. Prepare for basis-trade unwinds if Fed speakers surprise hawkish today.

*Sources: [[US Treasury yields](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/government-bond-yield)]([US Treasury yields](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/government-bond-yield)) · [[India G-Sec yield](https://tradingeconomics.com/india/government-bond-yield)]([India G-Sec yield](https://tradingeconomics.com/india/government-bond-yield)) · [[Fed inflation framework](https://www.frbsf.org/research-and-insights/publications/economic-letter/2026/03/fed-communications-and-inflation-expectations/)]([Fed inflation framework](https://www.frbsf.org/research-and-insights/publications/economic-letter/2026/03/fed-communications-and-inflation-expectations/)) · [[RBI policy stance](https://prsindia.org/policy/report-summaries/review-of-monetary-policy-framework-by-rbi)]([RBI policy stance](https://prsindia.org/policy/report-summaries/review-of-monetary-policy-framework-by-rbi))*

## 💹 Global Stocks

### NVDA (NASDAQ): -4.4% on May 15 as Trump-Xi summit ends with no H200 deal and China blocks purchases

NVDA closed Friday, May 15 at approximately $225.32, down roughly 4.4% from Thursday's close of $235.74, erasing approximately $170 billion in market value in a single session. The session opened down 3.0% and saw an intraday flash crash exceeding 4.6%. The catalyst was a two-part gut-punch: President Trump, returning on Air Force One after a two-day summit with Xi Jinping, told reporters that China "chose not to" approve purchases of the H200 chip because Beijing wants to develop domestic alternatives — this despite Washington having already cleared H200 sales to roughly ten Chinese firms including Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance, and JD.com. The selling was compounded by a hotter-than-expected April CPI print of 3.8% year-on-year, which sent the PHLX Semiconductor Index (SOX) down more than 3% on the session.

**Why it matters.** The failed H200 sales signal more than a missed quarter of revenue — they indicate Beijing is actively steering its largest technology buyers toward Huawei and domestic silicon, permanently narrowing the China total addressable market for NVDA's data center segment. Commerce Secretary Lutnick confirmed that Beijing blocked imports to accelerate domestic chipmaker development, which means the trade-policy window that Jensen Huang flew to Beijing to unlock has now closed structurally, not temporarily. For the AI semis complex, the implication is that China's AI buildout will run on a separate chip stack, bifurcating the global GPU market. For an AI engineer watching the semis complex, the near-term read-across is supply-side: if Chinese hyperscaler demand is absorbed by Huawei domestically, NVDA's next several quarters of data center guidance become a cleaner read on U.S. and non-China hyperscaler spend — useful signal for gauging whether the AI capex cycle is still accelerating or plateauing.

**Action.** NVDA reports Q1 FY2027 results after the close on Wednesday, May 20. Consensus EPS is $1.76 on roughly +118% earnings growth and +79% revenue growth year-on-year. The stock entered the weekend trading between $224.25 and $232.01 on May 16 with 37 analysts carrying a "Strong Buy" consensus and an average 12-month price target of $273.62. The 8-K and earnings transcript on May 20 will be the primary documents; focus on data center segment revenue, Q2 FY2027 guidance, and any explicit commentary on the H200 China channel and alternative market offsets. Nvidia IR filings are at investor.nvidia.com.

*Sources: [cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/14/trump-xi-meeting-china-stocks-ai-rally.html) · [tomshardware.com](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/trump-says-china-is-blocking-h200-purchases) · [tradingkey.com](https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-stocks/261900496-nvidia-stock-plunge-h200-china-sales-stall-trump-visit-domestic-chip-substitution-market-share-valuation-concerns-semiconductor-selloff-tradingkey)*

---

### CSCO (NASDAQ): +17.0% on May 14 after record $15.84B Q3 revenue and AI infrastructure orders guidance raised to $9B

Cisco Systems closed May 14 at approximately $116.54, up roughly 17% on the session following Q3 FY2026 results reported after the close on May 13. The company posted record quarterly revenue of $15.84 billion, up 12% year-on-year, with non-GAAP EPS of $1.06 against a consensus estimate of $1.00. The standout figure was on AI infrastructure orders: Cisco disclosed it has received $5.3 billion in AI infrastructure and hyperscaler orders year-to-date through Q3 FY2026 and raised its full-year AI order guidance to $9 billion from $5 billion — a 80% revision upward in a single quarter. Full-year FY2026 revenue guidance was raised to $62.8–$63.0 billion.

**Why it matters.** Cisco's breakout reframes the company from a legacy enterprise networking story into a direct AI infrastructure beneficiary, confirming that data center interconnect and networking fabric is a real and scaling bottleneck in the AI buildout. The $9 billion AI orders figure — and the stated target of at least $6 billion in AI hyperscale revenue in FY2027 — validates that hyperscalers are allocating serious capital to networking layers, not just compute. The $125 billion in combined Q1 2026 capex from the four major hyperscalers (Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft, Meta) needs to flow somewhere beyond GPUs, and Cisco's order book is one of the clearest public signals of where fabric and switching spend is going. For an AI engineer, the read-across is that inference-at-scale is straining network capacity in ways that are now showing up in vendor order books, not just conference keynotes. The product orders surge of 35% year-on-year also suggests this is not a one-quarter event.

**Action.** Cisco's Q3 FY2026 10-Q and earnings call transcript are the primary documents; the transcript detail on Silicon One ASIC deployments and the composition of the $5.3 billion in year-to-date AI orders (hyperscaler vs. enterprise vs. sovereign cloud) is the key read. Cisco IR is at investor.cisco.com. The stock is up 33% year-to-date heading into the weekend, outpacing the Nasdaq's 14% advance. Watch for any analyst price target revisions this week off the back of the May 14 move, and note that CSCO has historically given preliminary Q4 order color at industry conferences in June.

*Sources: [cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/13/cisco-csco-q3-earnings-report-2026.html) · [fool.com](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/05/14/why-cisco-systems-stock-jumped-17-today/) · [tradingkey.com](https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-stocks/261893167-cisco-q3-earnings-beat-ai-infrastructure-9b-guidance-silicon-one-job-cuts-restructuring-breakout-tradingkey)*

## 🏗 Tech & Startups

### Rapido Raises $240 Million at $3 Billion Valuation, Betting on India's Mobility Consolidation

Indian ride-hailing platform Rapido closed a $240 million primary funding round led by Prosus on May 15, 2026, hitting a $3 billion valuation. Existing investors WestBridge Capital and Accel also participated in what the company calls part of a larger $730 million financing exercise. The capital will expand Rapido's presence across high-growth markets and strengthen first- and last-mile connectivity, while the platform now supports over 9 million driver-partners across India.

**Why it matters.** Rapido's scale and Prosus's conviction signal that India's ride-hailing market is consolidating around genuine alternatives to Uber and Ola. At $3 billion, Rapido is well-funded enough to compete on reach while staying lean on unit economics—a playbook worth watching in markets where affordability matters. The $730 million total war chest suggests founders expect a prolonged competitive phase before dominance emerges. For builders, the lesson: in price-sensitive mobility, the player who can subsidize riders AND pay drivers fairly has optionality that pure venture returns can't force.

**Action.** Track Rapido's driver supply growth and geographic expansion across the tier-2/3 cities where Uber and Ola have limited reach. Monitor whether Prosus increases its stake in the next round (signaling conviction) or peels back (signaling discipline). Read Prosus's own announcement for how they frame India's digital economy bet post-2026.

*Sources: [[TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/15/indian-uber-rival-rapido-raises-240m-at-3b-valuation/)]([TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/15/indian-uber-rival-rapido-raises-240m-at-3b-valuation/)) · [[Prosus Official](https://www.prosus.com/news-insights/2026/prosus-leads-us-dollars-240m-primary-funding-in-rapido-demonstrating-conviction-in-indias-digital-economy)]([Prosus Official](https://www.prosus.com/news-insights/2026/prosus-leads-us-dollars-240m-primary-funding-in-rapido-demonstrating-conviction-in-indias-digital-economy)) · [[YourStory](https://yourstory.com/2026/05/rapido-raises-240m-in-primary-funding-led-by-prosus-at-3b-valuation)]([YourStory](https://yourstory.com/2026/05/rapido-raises-240m-in-primary-funding-led-by-prosus-at-3b-valuation))*

### Skyroot Aerospace Becomes India's First Spacetech Unicorn With $60 Million Round

Hyderabad-based launch startup Skyroot Aerospace raised $60 million on May 7, 2026, reaching a $1.1 billion valuation and claiming India's first space-tech unicorn status. The round was co-led by GIC, Singapore's sovereign wealth fund, and Sherpalo Ventures, with participation from BlackRock funds, Arkam Ventures, and founders of Greenko Group. Ram Shriram, founder of Sherpalo and Alphabet board member, joined Skyroot's board.

**Why it matters.** Skyroot's unicorn milestone validates a deeptech playbook: founders with domain expertise (aerospace engineers Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka) building orbital infrastructure at half the cost of legacy providers. The company doubled its valuation in 30 months—a pace that reflects genuine scarcity in smallsat launch capacity and India's emerging credibility in space. For builders, the signal is clear: infrastructure plays that solve unit economics and close real capability gaps can scale faster than consumer products. GIC's participation signals institutional LPs now see space-tech risk as acceptable.

**Action.** Watch Skyroot's Vikram-1 launch cadence and Vikram-2 development. The next story will be successful orbital flights and customer acquisition, not more capital raises. Track whether Indian defense and satellite operators commit launch orders (government de-risk) or international operators commit (market validation). Read Skyroot's own updates on manufacturing scale-up, the constraining variable for launch startups.

*Sources: [[Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/companies/news/skyroot-aerospace-raises-60-mn-becomes-a-unicorn-valued-at-1-1-bn-126050700238_1.html)]([Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/companies/news/skyroot-aerospace-raises-60-mn-becomes-a-unicorn-valued-at-1-1-bn-126050700238_1.html)) · [[DealStreet Asia](https://www.dealstreetasia.com/stories/skyroot-fundraise-481532)]([DealStreet Asia](https://www.dealstreetasia.com/stories/skyroot-fundraise-481532)) · [[Via Satellite](https://www.satellitetoday.com/finance/2026/05/07/skyroot-secures-60m-in-funding-becoming-indias-first-space-unicorn/)]([Via Satellite](https://www.satellitetoday.com/finance/2026/05/07/skyroot-secures-60m-in-funding-becoming-indias-first-space-unicorn/))*

## 🩹 Health & Science

### Phase 3 trial demonstrates RAS inhibitor daraxonrasib doubles survival in metastatic pancreatic cancer

A Phase 3 clinical trial of daraxonrasib, a novel RAS-targeted protein degrader, combined with chemotherapy doubled median overall survival in metastatic pancreatic cancer patients compared to chemotherapy alone, extending progression-free survival to 8-9 months on average. The drug represents a new class of genetically-engineered medicines targeting RAS mutations, which occur in 30-50% of pancreatic cancers.

**Why it matters.** Metastatic pancreatic cancer remains among the deadliest cancers with historical median survival under 12 months. Doubling survival time is a genuine inflection point, not incremental improvement. For AI-in-health developers, RAS mutation-driven response enables companion diagnostic applications and precision targeting. For clinical networks, this establishes a new standard-of-care pathway and creates immediate commercial opportunity in mutation screening and specialized oncology protocols.

**Action.** Oncology teams should review the NEJM publication for patient selection criteria and begin preparing RAS mutation screening infrastructure. Revolution Medicines is advancing this toward FDA submission. For diagnostics companies, high-throughput RAS sequencing becomes a critical clinical service line for precision pancreatic cancer management.

*Sources: [nbcnews.com](https://www.nbcnews.com/health/health-news/pancreatic-cancer-drug-daraxonrasib-doubles-survival-rcna343730) · [npr.org](https://www.npr.org/2026/05/12/nx-s1-5783797/pancreatic-cancer-breakthroughs-daraxonrasib-mrna)*

### Naked mole rat hyaluronic acid gene extends mouse lifespan 4.4% with multi-system rejuvenation

University of Rochester researchers transferred genes encoding hyaluronic acid synthesis from naked mole rats to laboratory mice, producing a 4.4% median lifespan extension alongside systemic reductions in inflammation, improved gut microbiota composition, and sustained tissue health during aging. The genetically modified mice demonstrated preserved metabolic function and resistance to age-related decline across multiple organ systems.

**Why it matters.** This demonstrates mechanistic causation—hyaluronic acid availability directly drives multi-system aging reversal including immune, metabolic, and microbial ecosystem improvements. For longevity researchers and therapeutics developers, the result validates HA metabolism as a causal target, suggesting non-genetic interventions like exogenous HA supplementation or HA-metabolizing bacterial strains could produce similar effects. The mechanism implicates microbiome composition as an actionable early biomarker for aging interventions.

**Action.** Researchers should immediately test exogenous hyaluronic acid supplementation or HA-metabolizing probiotic strains in mammalian models to confirm transferability outside genetic engineering. Clinical developers should integrate microbiome profiling into aging-intervention protocols as an early-stage efficacy marker. Supplement companies should prioritize HA-bioavailability formulations with microbiome-based validation endpoints for clinical credibility.

*Sources: [sciencedaily.com](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/05/260510030948.htm)*

## 🎤 Culture & World

### Bulgaria's Dara Wins Eurovision with "Bangaranga," Claiming First Victory in Contest History

Bulgaria won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on May 17 when Dara performed "Bangaranga" in Vienna, Austria. Israel's Noam Bettan finished second with "Michelle," a rock ballad sung in Hebrew, French, and English, while Romania's Alexandra Căpitănescu placed third with the rock song "Choke Me." The victory marks Bulgaria's first-ever win in the 70-year history of the competition.

**Why it matters.** Eurovision functions as a cultural barometer for European music consumption and soft power messaging. A historically peripheral nation claiming the top prize signals where musical influence is concentrating—away from traditional powerhouses and toward the periphery. This year's three-way race between ballads and dance-inflected pop suggests streaming platforms are fragmenting Eurovision's voting patterns by region, with older Eurovision formulas (emotionally direct ballads) still dominant but losing ground to uptempo production values Dara brought.

**Action.** Monitor whether "Bangaranga" charts outside Eurovision enthusiast circles on global streaming platforms. The test is whether a Eurovision victory translates to sustained Spotify rotation or remains contest-specific novelty. Check press for streaming details on Eurovision's distribution—where it airs determines whether younger audiences outside Europe encounter the contest as cultural moment or heritage broadcast.

*Sources: [[https://www.euronews.com/culture/2026/05/17/eurovision-2026-follow-our-coverage-of-the-worlds-greatest-song-contest](https://www.euronews.com/culture/2026/05/17/eurovision-2026-follow-our-coverage-of-the-worlds-greatest-song-contest)]([https://www.euronews.com/culture/2026/05/17/eurovision-2026-follow-our-coverage-of-the-worlds-greatest-song-contest](https://www.euronews.com/culture/2026/05/17/eurovision-2026-follow-our-coverage-of-the-worlds-greatest-song-contest))*

### Netflix's "Countdown: Rousey vs. Carano" Reunites MMA Icons for Comeback Fight on May 16

Netflix released "Countdown: Rousey vs. Carano" on May 16, a documentary following MMA legends Ronda Rousey and Gina Carano as they return to competition the same day. Uma Thurman narrates the eight-episode buildup, tracking both fighters' journeys back to professional mixed martial arts after years away from the sport.

**Why it matters.** Netflix mining legacy athlete comebacks signals how streaming platforms now function as nostalgia accelerators—betting that audiences will engage with decade-old celebrity narratives as entertainment product. Rousey and Carano represent the moment when women's MMA shifted from novelty to normalized sport. Their 2026 return asks whether that legitimacy has stuck or if interest was always tied to the singular draw of these two personalities. The documentary-plus-live-event strategy mimics traditional sports broadcasting but with Netflix's production overlay, testing whether algorithmic promotion can manufacture sporting drama as effectively as ESPN's decades-old infrastructure.

**Action.** Monitor fight ratings and streaming metrics—specifically whether the Netflix documentary drives viewers to the live bout, and whether combat sports viewership on Netflix sustains beyond this event. Track press coverage of the bout itself to gauge whether MMA media treats this as historic comeback or marketing stunt.
